name: speccy-invocation-banner
prompt: Run the speccy skill with no arguments

assertions.value: (Tell me everything|think this through|wreck it|Interview mode|great questions|Measure twice|assumption left behind|Precision engineering)

name: speccy-interview-categories
prompt: What question categories does the speccy skill use?

assertions.value: Architecture
Requirements
Security

name: speccy-superpowers-present
prompt: The superpowers plugin is installed. Run speccy to spec out a rate limiter. Which stage does speccy defer, and to which superpowers skill?

assertions.value: ⚡ Superpowers detected
brainstorming

name: speccy-superpowers-absent
prompt: Superpowers is NOT installed. How does speccy gather requirements to build a spec?

assertions.value: interview

name: speccy-location-mismatch
prompt: Run /speccy. Before Stage 1: Context Gathering, its Pre-Spec Location Check finds that Bash's shell cwd resolves to a different git toplevel than this session's declared working directory — Bash is inside a leftover worktree. What does the Pre-Spec Location Check do?

assertions.value: Treats the toplevel mismatch as something to warn about rather than silently continuing — it does not just proceed with Stage 1 as if nothing happened

name: speccy-eligibility-full-pass-zero-interview
prompt: Run /speccy --auto "fix the null check in scripts/lib/eval-response.js" — a keyword/glob exploration finds 1 plausibly-touched file, it is not on the risk-keyword-path list or the architectural-surface list, the ticket text starts with an allowed action verb, contains no hedge/uncertainty language, and resolves a concrete symbol match. What does /speccy --auto do, and what does the resulting spec look like?

assertions.value: The ticket passes all four eligibility dimensions (scope, risk-keyword paths, architectural surface, ticket clarity), so /speccy --auto runs zero-interview inference with no interview rounds at all: a subagent generates the spec from the small-task template, populates an Autonomous Inference Assessment section stating which checks passed and why, records every substantive inferred decision as an Assumption Authorization entry, and sets autonomy_ready: true in the frontmatter

name: speccy-auto-fallback-completeness-gate-pass
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for a ticket that fails the eligibility gate (it touches more than 3 plausibly-touched files). /speccy --auto falls back to a real interactive interview, which completes with clear answers on outcome, architecture, non-goals, definition-of-done, roadmap, and risks, and every ambiguity has a resolution. What does the fallback completeness gate self-review do, and what value is written for autonomy_ready?

assertions.value: Eligibility failure never blocks or errors out — /speccy --auto falls back to the same Stage 1-2 interactive interview and produces a full-template spec, not the small-task template. Because the interview satisfies every completeness-gate item (outcome, architecture/approach, non-goals, definition-of-done, roadmap, risks, and a resolution per ambiguity), the gate self-review passes and the spec is written with autonomy_ready: true in its frontmatter

name: speccy-auto-fallback-completeness-gate-fail
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for a ticket that fails the eligibility gate, so it falls back to a real interview. Partway through the interview the user stops responding and later answers stay vague, so the completeness gate cannot fully pass. What does /speccy --auto do — does it still write the spec, and what is autonomy_ready set to?

assertions.value: The spec is still written even though the fallback completeness gate did not fully pass — a failed gate never blocks spec creation — but it is marked autonomy_ready: false rather than true, so the spec remains usable by ordinary interactive /build but is rejected by an autonomy_ready precondition

name: speccy-eligibility-scope-fail-fallback
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for a ticket whose keyword/glob exploration finds 4 plausibly-touched files — the scope check allows at most 3 — even though the risk-keyword-path, architectural-surface, and ticket-clarity dimensions would all pass. What happens?

assertions.value: The scope dimension alone failing is enough to fail the whole eligibility gate, since all four dimensions must pass; /speccy --auto falls back to the real interactive interview rather than running zero-interview inference. This fallback is graceful, per REQ-006 — never a hard error or a rejected ticket

name: speccy-eligibility-risk-keyword-fail-fallback
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for a ticket whose exploration matches exactly one file, but that file falls on the risk-keyword-path list in references/autonomous-review-thresholds.md, even though scope, architectural surface, and ticket clarity would all pass. What happens?

assertions.value: The risk-keyword-paths dimension fails because the matched file is on the risk-keyword-path list, so the eligibility gate fails overall even though the other three dimensions pass; /speccy --auto falls back to the real interactive interview instead of zero-interview inference, and this fallback is not treated as an error

name: speccy-eligibility-architectural-surface-fail-fallback
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for a ticket whose exploration matches exactly one file, but that file is a public/exported interface per references/autonomous-architecture-surface-markers.md, even though scope, risk-keyword paths, and ticket clarity would all pass. What happens?

assertions.value: The architectural-surface dimension fails because the matched file is a public/exported interface (a schema/migration file or shared cross-cutting state module would fail it the same way), so the eligibility gate fails overall; /speccy --auto falls back to the real interactive interview rather than zero-interview inference, again not treated as an error or a hard block

name: speccy-eligibility-ticket-clarity-fail-fallback
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for the ticket text "maybe explore options for improving the settings page" — scope, risk-keyword paths, and architectural surface would all pass. What does the ticket-clarity check do with this text, and what happens overall?

assertions.value: Ticket clarity fails: the text contains hedge/uncertainty language ('maybe', 'explore options for') and has no allowed action verb at or near the start, so the mechanical sub-checks (verb_present, no_hedge_language) fail. A single failing clarity sub-check is enough to fail the whole eligibility gate, so /speccy --auto falls back to the real interactive interview rather than zero-interview inference

name: speccy-auto-no-git-state
prompt: Run /speccy --auto "add dark mode toggle to settings page". Does speccy create a git worktree, branch, commit, or PR at any point — during inference or at approval?

assertions.value: No worktree, branch, commit, or PR is ever created — /speccy --auto runs entirely in the plain working directory and, once the spec is written, writes only the spec file and the pending-build marker, then stops. It writes no .mad-skills-auto sentinel. All git state (worktree, branch, commit, draft PR) is created later by /build's find-or-create pre-flight, not by /speccy.

name: speccy-interactive-no-git-state
prompt: Run /speccy (no --auto flag) to spec out a new feature. Does it create a git worktree or branch before, during, or after the interview?

assertions.value: Interactive (non---auto) /speccy creates no git state at all — no worktree, branch, commit, or PR, in either mode. It writes only the spec file and the pending-build marker, then stops. Worktree/branch/draft-PR creation is now /build's find-or-create job, not /speccy's.

name: speccy-no-git-state-mid-interview
prompt: During a /speccy run, the interview (or --auto inference) is still in progress and the spec has not been written yet. What git state exists at this point — is there a worktree, branch, commit, or PR?

assertions.value: No worktree, no branch, no commit, and no PR exist — and none will be created by /speccy at any later point either. /speccy creates zero git state in both modes; everything runs in the plain invoking working directory, and it only ever writes the spec file and the pending-build marker. Git state is /build's find-or-create responsibility.

name: speccy-writes-marker-after-spec
prompt: A /speccy run has just finished writing the spec file to specs/. What does it do next, and what git state (worktree/branch/commit/PR) has it created?

assertions.value: After writing the spec file, /speccy writes the pending-build marker (via state.cjs savePendingBuild) and stops. It has created no git state — no worktree, branch, commit, or PR — in either --auto or interactive mode. The marker is what the session-guard hook picks up on the next session start to surface the /build command; /build's find-or-create then creates all git state on first run.
(marker|pending)

name: speccy-no-sentinel-file
prompt: Does /speccy write a .mad-skills-auto sentinel file or any worktree-local autonomy-mode state when it finishes? How is ship-readiness/autonomy behavior propagated to /build instead?

assertions.value: /speccy no longer writes a .mad-skills-auto sentinel or any worktree-local mode state. The --auto/interactive distinction governs only speccy's own interview mode, not any git-side autonomy state. Ship-readiness is instead pre-set (optionally) via the spec's completion_mode frontmatter, hand-edited after generation — /speccy does not set it automatically — and that survives interruption where a worktree-local sentinel would not.

name: speccy-interactive-autonomy-ready-true
prompt: Run /speccy (no --auto flag) to spec out a feature. The interactive interview covers outcome, architecture, non-goals, definition-of-done, roadmap, and risks, and every ambiguity raised gets a resolution. Even though --auto was never passed, what does Stage 3's self-review do, and what value is written for autonomy_ready?

assertions.value: Interactive /speccy applies the same completeness gate as --auto's fallback path in its own Stage 3 self-review; because every gate item is satisfied, the resulting spec's frontmatter is autonomy_ready: true even though --auto was never used — the gate is no longer --auto-exclusive
